title: Lesson Quiz: Career Clusters and Pathways
description: Assessment of the concept that Exploring career categories and industries.

choices.text: A group of related jobs and industries
choices.feedback: Correct. A career cluster is a broad category that includes related jobs and industries.

choices.text: One class a student takes in high school
choices.feedback: A class may support career learning, but it is not a career cluster.
An application form is not a career cluster.

choices.text: A list of personal hobbies only
choices.feedback: Hobbies may connect to careers, but they are not career clusters.

questions.prompt: A career pathway is a more specific route within a broader career cluster.

choices.feedback: A career pathway is a more specific area within a larger cluster.
Correct. Pathways help students explore more specific options inside a broad career cluster.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows a career pathway?

choices.text: A report card with semester grades
choices.feedback: A report card is not a career pathway.
A lunch menu does not describe a career pathway.

choices.text: A more focused area within a broad career field
choices.feedback: Correct. A pathway is a narrower route within a larger career cluster.
Weekend plans are unrelated to the idea of a career pathway.

questions.prompt: Why is learning about career clusters useful?

choices.text: It guarantees one final career choice right away
choices.feedback: Career clusters help with exploration, but they do not guarantee one final choice.

choices.text: It removes the need for self-assessment
choices.feedback: Self-assessment still matters when exploring careers.

choices.text: It helps students organize options and explore related careers
choices.feedback: This is close, but the best answer here is the clearer connection to exploring categories and industries.

choices.text: It helps students see how jobs can be grouped into related categories
choices.feedback: Correct. Career clusters help students understand how jobs connect across related industries.

questions.prompt: Career clusters and pathways can help students explore more than one possible future career option.

choices.feedback: Correct. Clusters and pathways help students compare several related options.
Career clusters are useful because they open up multiple connected options, not just one.
